dialog.stepLabel: Zani: "No one's been affected. Yes, no worries. Leave the rest to m..."

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: No one's been affected. Yes, no worries. Leave the rest to me.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Ah, it's you. What a coincidence.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: A short yet violent quake hit Ragunna not long ago. We still don't know the cause, but we're checking for casualties.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Fortunately, the damage seems minimal.

options.text: (Tell her about the Fabricatorium of the Deep.)

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: That explains the commotion. I didn't expect the Order to have a research facility hidden deep underwater.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Sounds like a whole mess to clean up there.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I'll report everything to Ms. Carlotta. With the Common Echoes involved, the families, the Order... No, the entire city will need to unite.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Thank you for bringing this information to us.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: If what Fenrico said is true, the Order should make the right choice here.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: But that's not something I need to worry about at my pay grade.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: If the situation in Septimont is still unclear, you should go there first. Don't worry about us. Ragunna will be fine.

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: Finally! {PlayerName}, we've been trying to reach you, but we kept losing the connection.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Are you alright?!

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You couldn't reach me? Must've been due to the chaotic frequencies at the seabed. Relax, I'm fine.

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: That's a relief. You mentioned the seabed. Have you discovered anything?

options.text: Yes, and I have something to say to Cartethyia.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: You were right. The Threnodian's power at the seabed came from Mya.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: It crystallized within a Gem, luring Fenrico to open a rift for the Dark Tide to seep through. It tried to escape back into the Tide to merge with the remaining Threnodian power to grow stronger.

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: You mean the <te href=851027>Threnodian's Gem</te> caused the Dark Tide? But we didn't detect any anomalous frequencies in Rinascita.

options.text: That's the very anomaly we're investigating.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: That's strange. The Dark Tide at the seabed... It's different from what I've seen before. In Ragunna, it always came from the sky.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Hmm… could you describe the Dark Tide you encountered in more detail?

options.text: It was like the crack in a shattered crystal.
It was an interior space where light and dark converged.
Swarms of Tacet Discords emerged from it.
Fenrico called it the Journeying Paradise.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I see... I'll need some time to think it through.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Sure. I'm heading to Septimont to follow the clues Fenrico left behind.

items.speaker: Black Shores Member
items.text: Please take care of yourself. We'll keep you updated with any developments.
